title: Purge by label-end (t_end_idx) overlap, not a fixed event-count scalar
source: LdP book audit (6 parallel read-only auditors vs AFML + Machine Learning for Asset Managers, books in D:/PROJECTS/QuantTradingDocs), 2026-06-03. Conversation: 'audita el proyecto usando los libros'.
why: make_folds (walk_forward.py:104-105) and PurgedTimeSeriesSplit (walk_forward.py:136-148) purge a FIXED scalar number of EVENTS (purge_bars=20) before each test, NOT by each training event's actual label-end t_end_idx. AFML 7.4.1 (Snippet 7.1 getTrainTimes) requires purging every train obs whose label window [t_i0,t_i1] overlaps the test span. Severity is event-density dependent: for sparse labeled primaries (momentum_zscore ~640 events / ~5000 bars => 20 events ~= 157 bars >> horizon 40) the fixed purge OVER-covers, so NO leak today. But _run_supervised_direct uses events=ALL in-scope bars (density ~1, one-bar-apart events, run_xau_d1.py:173,239) => 20 events ~= 20 bars < horizon 40 bars => REAL look-ahead leak of ~20 bars in every fold. The design is fragile: any denser primary or shorter horizon silently leaks. Inner CV PurgedTimeSeriesSplit has the SAME flaw AND no embargo at all.
value_rationale: Correctness/leakage. CRITICAL for supervised_direct mode (real leak), latent-fragile for sparse primaries. CLAUDE.md claim that inner-CV purges overlapping triple-barrier windows is only true by luck of event density, not by construction.
scope: Replace fixed-scalar purge with t_end_idx-based purge: drop any train event whose t_end_idx >= test window first BAR index
Apply the same label-end purge inside PurgedTimeSeriesSplit (inner CV) and add a post-val embargo for symmetry
Minimum stop-gap if full fix deferred: assert purge_bars >= horizon for any density>=~0.5 path; hard-fail supervised_direct otherwise
Add a regression test: a dense-event config must NOT retain any train event with t_end_idx inside the test span
recommendation: Implement label-end (t1) purge; it is robust across event densities and is the AFML-correct method. Treat supervised_direct as currently leaking until fixed.
out_of_scope: CPCV (tracked B0123). Embargo magnitude tuning beyond AFML ~0.01T.

history.reason: LdP book audit: purge not derived from label-end; leaks in dense-event mode

Implemented: make_folds + PurgedTimeSeriesSplit purge by label-end (t_end_idx) + inner-CV embargo; wired into run_xau_d1 (labeled+supervised_direct) and run_multi_h4. TDD, 24 walk_forward tests green, full suite green, dry-run clean.

chore(backlog): provenance for B0129-B0133 LdP-audit-fix commit
test(regression): re-anchor Phase 1 baseline to post-audit-fix numbers (B0129-B0133)
